title: Smartphone Ownership, Age of Smartphone Acquisition, and Health Outcomes in Early Adolescence.
C1_reason: Smartphone ownership and age of acquisition are focal predictors; tests associations with depression, obesity, and insufficient sleep. "smartphone ownership at age 12 was linked to higher risk for depression and insufficient sleep. Additionally, acquiring smartphones at younger ages correlated with obesity and sleep problems." "In Analysis 1, we tested the association of smartphone ownership (exposure 1) with health outcomes (depression, obesity, insufficient sleep) at the 2-year follow-up assessment (N=10,588)." "In Analysis 3...we then examined whether acquiring a smartphone by the 3-year follow-up (exposure 3) was associated with health outcomes measured at that same 3-year follow-up."

title: Developmental Pathways From Childhood Neurodevelopmental Traits to Early Adolescent Psychiatric Dimensions: The Role of Environmental and Lifestyle Factors.
C1_reason: Screen use (social media, passive watching, active gaming, social engagement) is tested as a mediator of neurodevelopmental traits → psychiatric outcomes. Mediation is a focal analytic role per FT-IC1. "we tested the role of putative environmental and lifestyle factors (parental warmth, parental monitoring, family conflict, school involvement, negative life events, neighborhood safety, sleep problems, physical activity, diet, screen use) as mediators of these longitudinal associations." "measures of screen use showed nonsignificant mediating effects when controlling for baseline screen use and mental health...This suggests that screen use is unlikely to lead to poor mental health outcomes in youth with neurodevelopmental traits." Screen use is explicitly modeled as a pathway variable linking neurodevelopmental traits to psychiatric dimensions — the null mediation result is still a tested association.
